title: verbascum thapsiforme subsp. densiflorum
intro: Verbascum thapsiforme subsp. densiflorum, also known as the dense-flowered mullein, is a biennial plant in the figwort family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant's health benefits include soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for respiratory issues and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Verbascum thapsiforme subsp. densiflorum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to alleviate respiratory issues.
